title: asplenium ruta-muraria subsp. pseudogermanicum
intro: Asplenium ruta-muraria subsp. pseudogermanicum is a rare and little-studied fern species, a subspecies of the wall-rue fern, typically found in mountainous regions and often growing on rocks and trees. The plant has been reported to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Asplenium ruta-muraria subsp. pseudogermanicum include potential uses in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, often in combination with other plants, to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
